claim: LLM evaluation can be made model-agnostic by separating task execution from judgment—use one model to run tasks and a different model as judge to avoid bias.
actionable: Design your benchmark to execute tasks with one model API, then independently run a separate judge model against the outputs with a predefined rubric.
source_title: How to Benchmark LLM Skills with an LLM-as-Judge

claim: LLM Skills Bench uses YAML-defined skill benchmarks and supports parallel API calls to test multiple models efficiently.
actionable: Use LLM Skills Bench (github.com/RitikPatill/llm-skills-bench) to define rubrics in config files and run benchmarks against OpenAI or Anthropic models in parallel.
source_title: How to Benchmark LLM Skills with an LLM-as-Judge

claim: A local JSON store without external databases or services is sufficient to persist evaluation results that can then be visualized in a web dashboard.
actionable: Store benchmark results in a local JSON file instead of setting up a database, then build a web dashboard that reads from that JSON store.
source_title: How to Benchmark LLM Skills with an LLM-as-Judge
